Mtn To Stay Civil Disco Pending Criminal Proceedings Against Him
Off Calendar Feb-02-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
Michael Lee, appeared for Defendant/moving party
John S. Chung, appeared for Plaintiff/opposing party

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's Motion To Stay Civil Discovery Pending Criminal Proceedings Against Him is Denied. There is no basis for a finding at this time that all or any discovery in this matter poses a threat of self-incrimination. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

(Re-Notice) Of Mtn To Invalidate Pltf'S Purported Service Of Dicso Request
Off Calendar Feb-02-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Michael Lee, appeared for Defendant/moving party
John S. Chung, appeared for Plaintiff/opposing party

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's Motion To Invalidate Plaintiffs' Purported Service of Discovery Requests is Granted in part and denied in part. The motion is granted as to Defendants Cheng & Associates LLC and GAIA Group LLC because counsel must have made an appearance before the court. Until counsel has made an appearance, service must be upon the party absent an agreement by the party.
The motion is denied as to Defendant Hsiao-Ping Cheng. The court finds that service was valid as the presumption of invalidity has been rebutted. Defendant Hsiao-Ping Cheng must respond to the discovery within 15 days of notice of this order. Objections have not been waived. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
